description_long: This object is a double-barreled shotgun with a weathered, rusted appearance, featuring two parallel cylindrical barrels with a turquoise patina and visible rust patches. The barrels are joined along their length and terminate in a circular muzzle, with the breech area showing a reddish-brown metallic receiver. A metallic, squared fore-end extends from the receiver, and a yellowish metallic tube or rod is visible beneath the barrels, likely representing a magazine or support. The trigger area is partially visible, with a simple, open trigger guard and a metallic trigger. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible stock, and the surface texture is rough and corroded, suggesting prolonged exposure to the elements.
description: A weathered, double-barreled shotgun with turquoise and rusted metal surfaces, lacking a visible stock and featuring a simple, open trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin barrels' circular openings, the squared fore-end, and the yellowish tube beneath the barrels.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the barrels, the rusted turquoise finish, and the squared metallic fore-end.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the breech ends of the barrels, the joined receiver, and the yellowish tube extending from the side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full length of the barrels, the open trigger guard, and the metallic trigger.
attribute_barrel_configuration: side-by-side double-barrel, both barrels of equal length with no visible ribbing or venting
attribute_trigger_guard_design: simple, open, rounded trigger guard with no reinforcement or finger grooves
